content: We can reframe graph anomaly detection as a self-supervised exploration task that treats rare anomalous subgraph structures as 'failed trajectories' of normal graph evolution, then use failed trajectory improvement frameworks to refine anomaly detectors by learning from misidentified anomalies. This turns unlabeled anomaly detection challenges into a self-improving cycle that leverages the abundant misclassification cases most methods discard.
source_topics: Self-supervised exploration strategy improvement from failed trajectories
